summary: Bug fix VERIFIED: User permissions save in Settings now works correctly. Tested: (1) Login as admin, (2) Navigate to Settings > Users, (3) Edit Staff User with RST Edit toggle, (4) Save - dialog closes, stays on Settings page, (5) Admin self-edit - works without redirect. All 7 backend API tests passed, all frontend UI tests passed.

bug_fix_verification.issue: User permissions save fails in Settings - page redirects to main page after save
root_causes_fixed: setUser prop was not passed from Settings to UsersTab component
localStorage was used instead of sessionStorage when updating admin's own permissions
can_edit_rst was missing from backend ROLE_PERMISSIONS

frontend_ui_tests.toggle_rst_edit_permission: PASS - toggled from unchecked to checked

code_review_verification.Settings.jsx_line_2804: UsersTab receives setUser prop correctly: <UsersTab user={user} setUser={setUser} />
code_review_verification.Settings.jsx_line_2371: Uses sessionStorage (not localStorage) for admin self-edit: sessionStorage.setItem('mill_user', JSON.stringify(updatedUser))
code_review_verification.auth.py_line_22: ROLE_PERMISSIONS includes can_edit_rst: True for admin role
code_review_verification.auth.py_line_28: ROLE_PERMISSIONS includes can_edit_rst: False for entry_operator role

context_for_next_testing_agent: Bug fix verified: User permissions save in Settings now works correctly. The fix involved: (1) passing setUser prop from Settings to UsersTab, (2) using sessionStorage instead of localStorage for admin self-edit, (3) adding can_edit_rst to backend ROLE_PERMISSIONS. Version is v88.45.0. Test file created: /app/backend/tests/test_user_permissions.py
